summary: Drew DeVault announces godocs.io as a community-maintained replacement for the sunsetting godoc.org, which Google is replacing with pkg.go.dev. The team forked the original codebase and cleaned it up significantly, removing Google-specific code, analytics, and dead features while rewriting the search index for Postgres. They commit to ongoing maintenance for users who prefer the original godoc.org experience. A note acknowledges the original version of the post was rewritten to remove unnecessary hostility.
key_points: Google is sunsetting godoc.org in favor of pkg.go.dev, prompting a community fork
The fork involved substantial cleanup: removing Google analytics, dead features, and reducing JavaScript requirements
The search index was rewritten for Postgres, decoupling it from Google infrastructure
DeVault later rewrote the announcement to remove what he acknowledged was unnecessary salt toward Google
money_quotes: We have forked the codebase and cleaned things up quite a bit, removing lots of dead or obsolete features, cleaning out a bunch of Google-specific code and analytics, reducing the JavaScript requirements, and rewriting the search index for Postgres.
We will commit to its maintenance going forward for anyone who prefers the original godoc.org experience over the new website.
Notice: this article was rewritten on 2021-01-19. The original article has a lot of unnecessary salt.
key_insight: When a corporation abandons community-valued infrastructure, the open source community can fork and maintain it independently, free of corporate dependencies.
